subject: Economics
chapter: Employment: Growth, Informalisation and Other Issues

formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's the complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Economics - Employment: Growth, Informalisation and Other Issues:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Lewis Model Formula",
    "formula": "N = N_L + N_S",
    "variables": "N: Total Labour Force, N_L: Labour Force in the Modern Sector, N_S: Labour Force in the Traditional Sector",
    "units": "Persons",
    "notes": "This model assumes that the traditional sector has a fixed supply of labour and that the modern sector has an unlimited supply of labour."
  },
  {
    "name": "Dual Labour Market Theory",
    "formula": "Wage Gap = (W1 - W2) / W1",
    "variables": "Wage Gap: Difference in wages between the Modern and Traditional Sectors, W1: Wages in the Modern Sector, W2: Wages in the Traditional Sector",
    "units": "Ratio",
    "notes": "This theory explains the wage difference between the modern and traditional sectors."
  },
  {
    "name": "Structural Unemployment",
    "formula": "Structural Unemployment = (L - E) / L",
    "variables": "Structural Unemployment: Unemployment due to mismatch between the available skills and the required skills, L: Labour Force, E: Employed Labour",
    "units": "Ratio",
    "notes": "This formula calculates the percentage of structural unemployment in the labour market."
  },
  {
    "name": "Frictional Unemployment",
    "formula": "Frictional Unemployment = (L - E) / L",
    "variables": "Frictional Unemployment: Unemployment due to the time taken for workers to find new jobs, L: Labour Force, E: Employed Labour",
    "units": "Ratio",
    "notes": "This formula calculates the percentage of frictional unemployment in the labour market."
  },
  {
    "name": "Natural Rate of Unemployment (NRU)",
    "formula": "NRU = Structural Unemployment + Frictional Unemployment",
    "variables": "NRU: Natural Rate of Unemployment, Structural Unemployment: Unemployment due to mismatch between the available skills and the required skills, Frictional Unemployment: Unemployment due to the time taken for workers to find new jobs",
    "units": "Ratio",
    "notes": "This formula calculates the natural rate of unemployment, which is the equilibrium level of unemployment in the labour market."
  },
  {
    "name": "Informalisation",
    "formula": "Informalisation = (L - E) / L",
    "variables": "Informalisation: Percentage of workers in the informal sector, L: Labour Force, E: Employed Labour",
    "units": "Ratio",
    "notes": "This formula calculates the percentage of workers in the informal sector of the economy."
  }
]
```

Note that this chapter primarily deals with concepts and theories rather than mathematical formulas. The formulas provided above are based on the theories and concepts discussed in the chapter.
